decisions.situation: Choosing between going to Texas vs staying in Ohio
decisions.reason: Better to deadhead to a good location like Ohio than go to Texas with bad outbound options
decisions.quote: просто виїхати в правильну локацію і краще стояти десь в Огайо день а не їхати в Техас

pricing_rules: Minimum rate target is around $1.40-$1.50 per mile
In California, rates around $1/mile may be acceptable
Dallas is a good location on Monday, so can accept cheaper rates
From Florida it's hardest to leave - take any money to get out
Short local loads in the evening can pay $400-$700
When stuck in bad location before Friday, take any rate to move
Owner offering $2/mile in a good location is considered too low
RPM of ~$3.03 for short hauls (e.g. Erie PA to Lawrence MA) is a golden load range
Rate around $2/mile to Utah is a reasonable ask
1300 for 1045 miles (~$1.24/mile) is considered weak but may need to take it
Below $1.70 RPM is very cheap - minimum $1.70 for such loads
$1.20 RPM to South Dakota is too cheap
$1.40 RPM is acceptable only if load is 1000+ miles, and even then think twice
$1500 is market price for certain mid-range loads
$1.65 RPM with Monday delivery is not serious/too low
When offering rate to broker, 95% chance they give the load
For owner-operators: take load for e.g. $2000 and sell for $1600 minimum, keep 25% of profit, but must be at least 10% profit from total load value
Alternative for owner-operators: 2% from gross like company trucks
Average RPM for hotshot is 1.5-2.5
For 26ft truck, current loads run close to $2 RPM
Below $4300 is too cheap for certain 26ft loads
If posted at 1.25 per/mile, negotiate up to around 1.6
1.46 per/mile is noted as a reference point
Deadhead must be factored into rate evaluation
Delivery timing (e.g., Tuesday delivery) affects rate assessment
Below $1.80 RPM is considered cheap/undesirable
$1800 for a load requiring $100 deduction is too cheap
Florida outbound loads at $1/mile are not profitable
Loads 1000+ miles preferred
Target minimum around $700+ for NJ area loads
2.21 per mile is considered a top rate
1.37 per mile is acceptable only when driver has been idle too long
6000 lbs is maximum critical weight, lighter is better
Canada rates are normal/good
Rate should cover the cost of return trip
$1/mile is the absolute minimum, only to reposition trucks
Below $1.45/mile is considered low
$1.58/mile noted as a specific rate reference point
Always add 10% markup to broker's rate
Don't go too cheap on Kentucky loads
Around $100 per load is acceptable for short/small loads
$1/mile loads lead to cancellations and delays - risky
Spot rate to Nebraska is $1.11 RPM — considered low, carriers don't want to go
Below $1/mile rates are common but bad — 'навіть по долару немає шоб в кращу локацію переїхати'
Partials can supplement low RPM loads — 'навіть якщо груз по баксу то паршал вас легко виручить'
On Fridays brokers may accept lower prices on long-distance loads
Bogdan's truck max weight is 5000-5500 lbs, own trucks can take 6000 lbs
Hotshot loads can pay better for box trucks — worth calling on them
$1.82 RPM is considered a normal/acceptable rate
New York loads must be at least $2/mile
$300 for 50 miles (~$6/mile) is a good short haul rate
$500 for 1000 miles ($0.50/mile) is very low — noted as a data point
Target gross revenue per truck is $5-6K per week
Day when load is dropped off does not count as a working day
Will cut a few bucks from own rate rather than fight with a new broker on first load
Minimum rate is $1.5 RPM
D-rated brokers are not acceptable unless critical
Yellow-rated brokers are not acceptable
Ryan (broker) always sends first rate minus $100
$1.4 RPM is considered too low (not worth it with deadhead)
$1.2 RPM is questionable, may need to wait for better
Below $1.80 RPM is implied bad based on context of rejections
$1.28 RPM is too low - load rejected at that rate
$1.4 RPM is questionable - needs review
Truck downtime costs -$150/day per driver
On Fridays, prioritize loads 1000+ miles
300-400 mile loads on weekends are undesirable
Nailed-to-floor securement loads must be extremely high-paying to justify
If struggling to find loads, lower price rather than let trucks sit idle over weekend
Name your own rate to brokers - they often agree: 'Кажеш свій рейт вони часто соглашаються'
Target RPM is $1.30-$1.50+ per mile; $1.20 RPM only covers costs and is not profitable
Competitors with similar brokers achieve $1.50-$1.70 RPM per week; team currently at ~$1.20 RPM
Partials (partial loads) significantly improve RPM - but hard to get when loading at 2-6 PM
Some loads offered at $1/mile are noted as bad, especially to poor locations
Broker compensation for extras must be added to the rate confirmation document

rules: Always call on loads even without listed dimensions - might be something small that fits at a good price
Broker must hear what they want to hear - manage communication strategically
When explaining rate decisions, always state your reasoning
Check if load fits truck before calling (14ft box truck / Conestoga limitations)
Must verify loads through factoring before accepting from new/unknown brokers
Send rate confirmations to quotes@212expedite.com
Before booking, confirm load is guaranteed
Must have $700,000 cargo insurance for certain loads
OK with Macropoint tracking
On Fridays, priority is to get out of bad locations at any price
Evening loads (short distance) can pay $400-$700 - worth calling on
PO (Power Only) has two styles: Drop&Hook and Loadout - know the difference
DAY LOADOUT means you take a trailer and haul with it for 1-2 weeks then deliver it to destination
Must be set up/registered with brokers before booking loads
Use Sylectus and DAT for load boards; always keep refreshing DAT if it glitches
Three drivers have different strategies: Igor 600-800 miles, Volodya local to Indiana, Mykhailo long distance anywhere
Only take full loads, no partials: 'паршала не шукай, нас тут цікавлять тільки фулл лоади'
Always introduce yourself on calls: 'Hello, this is Andrew with 212 Expedite, calling about your load'
Always verify exact dimensions before committing to avoid wasted trips: 'дізнайся точні дімси, щоб дарма не їхати завтра'
Send BOL to broker after loading
Confirm everything with broker before booking: 'букай, тільки підтвердь з брокером все-все-все'
Call flatbed and hotshot loads and ask dimensions - propose our truck if it fits
Enter loads into the spreadsheet after driver is loaded
Don't give cheap rates - minimum $1.70 RPM for standard loads
Be proactive - call maximum, don't miss loads: 'мені потрібно від тебе більше включення, дзвони максимально, старайся не пропускати вантажі'
For owner-operators: minimum 10% profit margin on load resale, dispatcher gets 25% of profit; or 2% of gross
Owner-operator (16ft box, up to 5500 lbs) is not exclusive - other companies also load him
Some customers require minimum 10 delivered loads before working with you
When you offer a rate to a broker, 95% chance they give the load - so be careful with offers
Need minimum 10 loads on CH Robinson before accessing premium loads
Only search for next load AFTER driver has loaded current one
If brokers are stalling, send them the scheme/documentation
Call for loads rather than waiting for email/text responses on sketchy loads
For loads posted for 53ft trucks, sometimes don't disclose 26ft truck if dimensions clearly fit
For box truck loads, disclose box truck upfront
Always fight aggressively for loads - experienced reps will take them if you hesitate
Truck weight capacity up to 12,000 lbs
Trucks cannot be loaded via ramps
Get broker agreement/confirmation first before proceeding
On Monday (US holiday), long-haul loads will go fast - plan Friday accordingly
Ask broker for pickup number; sometimes it's in the confirmation
Always send POD to broker after delivery
Introduce/brief the driver about the load details
Account for deadhead and delivery day when evaluating rate
All loads should be 1 pickup 1 drop only - avoid multi-drop loads
Always verify loads from Landstar brokers
Maximum critical weight is 6000 lbs, lighter is better
Loads must be 1000+ miles preferred
Monitor and control driver loading/unloading schedules
Don't let drivers sit idle too long - give them work even at lower rates if necessary
When calling brokers early (around 7am), expect low answer rates
Food commodities (chips, olive oil) may not be loaded for their trucks
Calculate carefully if driver can make delivery on time - being late causes problems
On hot loads, call immediately - don't rely only on email
Cover return trip costs in the rate
Before holidays/weekends expect cheap rates - plan accordingly
Dispatchers get commission split on loads they work on
Add MC number to your email signature to speed up broker communication
Give broker updates every 15 minutes during pickup/delivery
Before updating broker, check with manager if anything changed
Always enter all loads into the tracking spreadsheet/table
Upload POD and close out loads after unloading
Check broker MC on RTS before taking loads
Always add 10% markup to broker's rate
Watch loads in real-time, especially in the first half of the day - good loads can be taken within 30 seconds
On Sylectus, once you bid you must take it - cannot refuse
Focus on DAT as primary load board, Sylectus deprioritized
Upload rate confirmations to the shared drive
Log all loads into the new month's sheet (e.g., October)
Don't skip loads on Sylectus board
Learn to work with one truck at a time to not miss loads
On Fridays, manager also searches loads - dispatchers should be competitive
Use LockTheLoad extension - double-click load to pull RTS rating
Do NOT cancel loads — cancellations lead to reports and damage company reputation: 'другий кенсел від нас за сьогодні то не серйозно взагалі'
Carefully verify loads before booking to avoid cancellations: 'уважно пеервіряйте що ви берете, щоб нам не кенселити'
When truck is loaded, remove from today and mark as uncovered for tomorrow: 'коли трак загрузився, то ви видаляєте його з сьогоднішнього дня та позначаєте, що на завтра він ще не покритий'
Check BOL photos quality — blurry drop photos won't pass factoring: 'на дроп такі розмазані фотки ен прокатять, фаторинг верне мені'
Use new email quotes@212expedite.com as primary for all boards
All new loads go on a new page — mark loads you're managing with docs and your name
Don't add loads into David's chain — keep things organized separately
Discuss loads in group chat, not private messages: 'краще пишіть в чат, щоб порадитись за вантаж, не мені в приватні'
Update all data on CH Robinson (Navisphere) site promptly or rating drops and loads get denied
Give broker ETAs immediately, update status on broker websites: 'які тільки приїхав пишіть брокеру й оновлюйте на сайті'
After booking a load: give broker ETA, add tracking, write load name and comments in TRACKING 212 EXPEDITE chat, tag tracker, add to spreadsheet, then search for next load
Add tracking@212expedite.com to chain for all booked loads and make sure they reply to all
If doubts about broker's MC or they are D-rated, call RTS at 913.329.8068
Hotshot loads are worth calling on with box trucks — can pay better
Bogdan's truck max weight 5000-5500 lbs, own trucks can handle 6000 lbs
Driver must notify broker 30 minutes before going into detention to be eligible for detention pay
Try to get Friday driver loads that allow them to go home over the weekend: 'аби так, щоб він міг заїхати додому'
Partials should be used to supplement low-rate loads
Don't put pick up/delivery times or rates in the group chat with drivers — it interferes with driver management and costs money
Don't ignore brokers — always respond
When a driver has tight timing, dispatcher and tracker must monitor and ensure driver makes the appointment; if running late, notify both broker and shipper proactively
Non-factored loads can only be accepted in one specific format
Use Truckstop loadboard in addition to DAT, especially in late hours when DAT has fewer loads
All loads must be logged in the new tracking page/spreadsheet
When covering your own trucks, you can take loads assigned to other dispatchers' trucks if they are already covered
Conestoga bid photos should show empty trailer (not loaded) to avoid brokers thinking it's a partial load request
Day load is dropped off does not count toward driver working time
Training/education sessions are mandatory attendance
$200 referral bonus for recommending a dispatcher or recruiter who stays for one month
If driver misses appointment, absorb the cost internally on first loads with new brokers rather than confronting the broker
Open and review every load posting — don't skip loads, good ones can be hidden among bad-looking postings
Market conditions: November showed ~10-15% drop in load volume (from 4200-4400 to 3700-3900 on Selectus) — expect tighter market
Brokers have become very picky in the last 3 years since the market dropped — history matters for getting loads
D-rated brokers are not accepted unless critical situation
Yellow-rated brokers are not accepted
Minimum RPM target is $1.5
Signs (company signs on trucks) must be verified before pickup and removed after leaving pickup, same for delivery - priority #1
Owner operators must check in under company MC but drive under their own authority
Always verify suspicious brokers by calling their company
Communicate with brokers carefully - soft, with explanations, no unnecessary words or emotions
Always provide broker updates, especially overnight status
When a screenshot with a number is shared, it means call that number
After taking a load, get set up with the broker immediately
Photos must be sent to broker urgently when issues arise
Dispatchers and tracking both responsible for sign compliance
Do not make driver look like a liar to brokers - be diplomatic about delays
For owner-operator loads, company documents must be on cargo paperwork
Advances of $300 distributed to team, remainder paid on 2nd-7th of month
Dispatcher is the primary person responsible for pickup and delivery, including after-hours: 'В першу чергу саме диспетчер відповідальний за завантаження в робочий так й позаробочий час'
If truck loads/unloads during non-working hours, notify tracking (Vadim) in advance: 'якщо у вас хтось грузиться в інший час повідомте Вадима'
Downtime is counted from the next day after unloading: 'Простой рахується з наступного дня після розвантаження'
Day when load was dropped but no cargo available does not count as downtime: 'День коли скинув, але не буде вантажу не враховується'
Tell brokers we have 5 trucks: 'якщо що то для брокерів у нас 5 траків'
Do not take TQL loads currently: 'А грузи TQL ми поки не беремо'
On Fridays prioritize 1000+ mile loads
Avoid leaving drivers stranded over weekends - lower price if needed rather than have trucks sit idle
Nailed-to-floor loads only if extremely high-paying
Name your own rate to brokers - they often agree
Drivers must speak at least some English or risk broker cancellation
NEVER give VIN number to broker without manager approval: 'В ЖОДНОМУ РАЗІ НЕ ДАВАТИ VIN БРОКЕРУ БЕЗ МЕНЕ!'
All rate confirmations must be forwarded to quotes@212expedite.com
Any extra compensation agreed with broker must be added to the rate confirmation document
Update the tracking table after every delivery - don't leave it unupdated
Confirm with broker after driver drops off a load
Add tracker (Vadim) to all load-related conversations
Maintain a blacklist tab for brokers/entities to avoid
All load-related questions should be posted in the group chat, not in private messages to manager
Dispatchers should help each other and make their own decisions - enough expertise exists in the team
Owners cannot share VIN numbers directly with brokers - only management provides the correct VIN
80% of loads sourced from DAT, 10% Sylectus, 10% broker-offered loads
Avoid delivering to military bases (carousel/kernel loads)
Driver must take photos at service stops as proof for broker
Provide frequent updates to broker to prevent load cancellation
January-February are the hardest months for rates; it gets easier after
Prioritize good outbound locations over cheap immediate loads - avoid getting stuck in bad areas
Partials are key to increasing RPM - try to load earlier in the day to have a chance at partials
Watch for double brokering (e.g., Landstar suspected)
